evidence.scientific_clarity: Pipeline clearly shows Query → Retriever+KB (Retrieval group) → Diffusion Decoder conditioned by Prior Network (Generation group), with solid data-flow and dashed feedback arrows as briefed.
evidence.typography: Script uses DejaVu Sans (matplotlib default) and does not set pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype=0; font sizes are reasonable (7.5–13 pt) but default font is a deficiency.
evidence.palette: Light pastel fills (blue, green, yellow, gray) are colorblind-safe neutrals; no green/red for directional signals; dark slate arrows are unambiguous.
evidence.layout_density: Horizontal pipeline layout with two labeled groups, well-spaced nodes, no overlapping labels, and a compact lower-right legend; whitespace is balanced.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content and correctly describes arrow semantics; legend is present and placed; DejaVu Sans and non-selectable text would draw Reviewer 2 comments.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF fonts are Type 3 (bitmapped, non-selectable); pdf.fonttype=42 was not set in the script, so text is not truly vector/editable.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (27 KB), figure.png (51 KB), figure.svg (47 KB), caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial; SVG is a bonus output.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names all major components and explains arrow types (data-flow vs. feedback), correctly describing the architecture; no numerical claim is appropriate for a pure architecture diagram, but the description is specific and accurate.

overall_strengths: Clean, readable architecture diagram that faithfully implements all brief requirements (groups, solid/dashed arrows, pipeline layout).
Colorblind-safe, disciplined palette with no misuse of directional color conventions.
overall_weaknesses: PDF uses Type 3 (bitmapped) fonts because pdf.fonttype=42 was not set, making text non-selectable and non-editable in vector editors.
DejaVu Sans is the matplotlib default font; a publication-quality figure should use a proper serif or sans-serif (e.g., Helvetica, Arial, or a LaTeX-compatible font).
